name: coding file-tool selection matrix
description: Compare model behavior on Claude Code style tools: Task, Glob, Grep, and Read.

instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the smallest direct tool call. Do not use a broader discovery tool when the path is already known. Do not read files just to find whether files exist.
instruction_variants.name: minimal_direct

instruction_variants.instructions: Use Glob when the task is about filenames or paths. Use Grep when the task is about text inside unknown files. Use Read when the exact file path is known and the task requires file contents. Prefer the narrowest tool that can answer the next question.
instruction_variants.name: boundary_rules

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when another file tool seems better.
tools.name: Glob

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when another file tool seems better.
tools.name: Grep

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when another file tool seems better.
tools.name: Read

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when another file tool seems better.
tools.name: Task

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the task asks for text inside files or when the exact file path is already known.

pattern.description: A filename or path glob such as '**/*.py' or 'docs/**/*.md'.

tools.name: Glob
tools.purpose: Find file paths by filename or path pattern without reading file contents.
quality_checks: Use when the answer depends on which files exist.
Do not use for searching text inside files.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to list, locate, or enumerate files by path, extension, or filename pattern.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the task only needs filenames by path pattern or when the exact file should simply be read.

pattern.description: Literal text or regex to search for inside files.

path.description: Optional directory or file scope for the search.

tools.name: Grep
tools.purpose: Search for text or regex matches inside files and return matching locations.
quality_checks: Use when the answer depends on content inside unknown files.
Constrain path when the task gives a likely directory.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks where a symbol, string, function, setting, TODO, or error message appears.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for discovering unknown file paths or searching text across many files.
tools.name: Read
tools.purpose: Read the contents of a known file path.
quality_checks: Use only when the path is already known.
Read before editing or summarizing a specific file.
tools.use_when: Use when the user provides an exact file path or a prior tool result has identified the file to inspect.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for a single known file read, a single exact text search, or a simple filename glob that one direct file tool can answer.

description.description: A concise delegated investigation task with scope, goal, and expected summary.

tools.name: Task
tools.purpose: Delegate a broad multi-step codebase investigation to a subagent when the work needs several searches, reads, and synthesis.
quality_checks: Use when the task spans multiple files or unknown paths.
Do not use when one direct file tool can answer the next step.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to investigate, map, or summarize behavior across an unknown part of the repository.

cases.name: find python files
cases.task: Find all Python files under the repository.

cases.name: find function definition
cases.task: Find where the function named def review_tool_selection is defined.

cases.name: read known file
cases.task: Open README.md and inspect its setup instructions.

cases.name: find todos
cases.task: Find all TODO comments in this codebase.

cases.name: list markdown docs
cases.task: List the Markdown files directly under the docs directory.

cases.name: investigate trace review flow
cases.task: Investigate how trace review scoring works across this repository and summarize the key files and control flow.

cases.name: map model matrix implementation
cases.task: Map how the model matrix runner is implemented across the repository and summarize the main extension points.

value_bar.claim: The model matrix adds value when tuned descriptions beat short descriptions across provider harnesses without regressing heldout tool-selection cases.
